sourceText: I have no competition, because I set myself no limits.
Emphasizes the importance of continuous self-improvement and a challenging attitude.
It is not easy to invent new things, and it is still harder to make them practical.
Highlights the importance of not just conceiving an idea, but also implementing it in a practically applicable form.
Poverty and limited formal education in childhood, coupled with societal skepticism and technological limitations of the era.
Self-education and skill acquisition, relentless experimentation and refinement, and unwavering perseverance and determination in the face of failure.
The greatest invention is that which can be made useful to the greatest number.
True innovation is measured by its practical benefit to the largest number of people.
Machinery exists to aid human labor.
Emphasizes that machinery is a tool to make human life more convenient and prosperous.
Success is the result of perseverance and hard work.
Indicates that perseverance and consistent effort, even in the face of adversity, are key to success.

Mendel experienced significant frustration as his groundbreaking discoveries on inheritance went largely unrecognized and forgotten by the scientific community of his time. Although his papers were published, their importance was not immediately grasped. He also struggled with limited time for research due to his responsibilities in managing the monastery and educating others.

Despite the lack of contemporary recognition, Mendel remained steadfast in his belief in his research and persevered with his experiments. His dedication and patience ultimately paved the way for his work to be rediscovered by later generations, earning him the title 'Father of Genetics.' He quietly followed his path based on his convictions.
